sutta: [Music] is 1 of the well-known suitazer once when the exalted 1 was dwelling near raja gaha on mount valchar peak now the vulnerable sauna in solitude apart thought thus in his mind the exalted 1's disciples live in active energy and i am 1 of them yet my heart is not detached and free of the ask of us my family is rich and i can enjoy riches and do good what if i were to forsake the training and turn to lower things enjoy riches and do good and the exalted 1 knowing in his own heart the vulnerable sonar's thoughts as a strong man might stretch his bent arm or bend his stretch um left mound vulture peak and appeared in whole wood before the venable sauna and when his seat was ready the exalted 1 sat down and the bamboo stone after saluting also sat down at 1 side and the exalted 1 said to him so seated so now did you not thus think in your heart the exalted ones disciples live in active energy and i am 1 of them yet my heart is not detached and free of the ask of us what if i were to forsake the training and turn to lower things yes lord what thank you sona were you not in the old days at home clever at the loots string music yes lord and what thank you sona when your loots strings were over strung was your loon then tuneful and playable no indeed lord and what thank you zona when your loot strings were overlapped was your loot then tuned full and playable no indeed lord but when sauna your loot strings were neither over strung nor overlaps but key to the middle pitch was at loot then tuned full and playable surely lord even so sona energy when overstrung ends in flurry or restlessness and overlaps in idleness wherefore sona stand fast in the mean of energy pierce the mean in the use of the faculties and therein grasp the real worth yes lord and the exalted 1 after charging the winner persona with this council as a strong man might bend his arm to and fro left cool wood and appeared on mon mount vulture p and presently the wearable sonar stood fast in the mean of energy pierced the mean in the faculties and grasped therein the mark and living alone secluded earnest arden resolute entered and abort not long after here amid things seen by his own power in the realization of that and above all of the holy life for the sake of which clansmen rightly go forth from the home to the homeless life and he knew birth is ended the holy life lived done is what was to be done there is no more of this to come and the wearable sonar was numbered among the arahants then having 1 to our handship the verbal sonar thought suppose i visit the exalted 1 and declare knowledge near him and he went to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated he said lord the arahant monk who has destroyed the earth of us lived the life done what was to be done set down the burden 1 self-will shattered lives better and is freed by perfect knowledge has applied himself to 6 things to dispassion detachment harmlessness destroying craving destroying grasping and to non-delusion perhaps lord some verbal person may think could it be that this verbal man has applied himself to dispassion relying on mere faith alone let him not think so lord the asava free monk who has lived the life done what was to be done who ceased not in himself to be done not to be added to what has been done by the fact of being passionless has applied himself to dispassion by destroying passion by the fact of being without hatred has applied himself to dispassion by destroying hatred by the fact of being delusion has applied himself to dispassion by destroying delusion or he may think could it be that this verbal man has applied himself to detachment while hankering after gains favors and flattery to harmlessness while backsliding from the true clinging to rules and rituals to destroying craving to destroying grasping to non-delusion while clinging to rules and rituals let him not think so lord the asava freed monk by the fact of being without passion hatred delusion has applied himself to detachment harmlessness destroying craving destroying grasping non-delusion by destroying passion hatred and delusion lord if objects cognizable by the eye come very strongly into the range of vision of a monk wholly freed in mind they obsess not his mind and his mind is untroubled firm having 1 to composure and he marks their said so too sounds cognizable by the ear smells by the nose stays by the tongue contacts by the touch and thoughts by the mind he mouths their setting imagine lord a mountain crank or rock cleftless chasm-less massive and a squall were to come very strongly from the east he would not shake nor rock noster that crack over a squall to come from the west from the north from the south it would not shake nor rock nor stir it even so lord if objects cognizable by the eye come very strongly into the range of the monk's vision 1 wholly freed in mind they obsess not his mind and his mind is untroubled firm having 1 to composure and he marks their set their setting so too of sounds smells taste touches and thoughts that's the end of the suta
commentary: this is 1 of the well known sutas and it's also mentioned in the vinaya about this monk this month is called sona kolivisa and he was supposed to come to come from a very rich family and was so uh so delicate that it seemed that the book at the source of his feet and at the bottom of his feet now hair grew and uh it was that is what is stated in the books and after he became a monk he was very diligent in practicing and he tried not to sleep at night and he whenever he was tired or sleepy he would walk and he walked so much that his delicate feet broke man sort of was cut and he bled and he bled so much and that in the morning when they look at the place where he walked meditation uh he was so um bloodstained that they thought he was like somebody had just slaughtered a cow or something there and in spite of all his hard work he was not yet an arahant so because of putting too much energy he was he lost heart and he thought of this robin so the buddha came to him and told him that as a layman he used to play the guitar so the buddha said if the guitar strings were too tight it would not sound well and it was too loose also would not sound loud but it was tuned to the middle picture then it would sound very well so the buddha advised him to practice the middle path and he did so and soon enough he became an arahant and when he became an arahant like those other arahanza they don't come and say that i have become an arahant they just declare knowledge by saying the how uh arahanna sees things as they really are now the next 2 thai
